concerns.text: Linear mixed-effects models with only 5 datasets as random effects groups is statistically unsound. Random effects typically require 10+ groups for reliable variance component estimation. Consider fixed-effects regression or increasing dataset count.

Class imbalance ratio and base rate are mathematically related (imbalance = max(P(Y=1),P(Y=0))/min(P(Y=1),P(Y=0)), base rate = P(Y=1)). Using both as predictors creates multicollinearity risk. VIF diagnostics mentioned in edge cases but should be explicit in FR-006 methodology.

SC-004 requires multiple-comparison correction (Bonferroni/Benjamini-Hochberg) but FR-005 only mandates computing correlations with p-values. FR-005 should explicitly require applying correction to all correlation tests.

Unresolved placeholder markers present: '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION: does <dataset> contain <variable>?]' appears in US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3 and Edge Cases. These should be resolved before spec finalization.

Base rate difference and demographic parity difference have a theoretical relationship (perfect calibration links them). While not circular, the spec should acknowledge this relationship in interpretation guidance per Constitution Principle VII.
